note: Round-1 persona attack notes (condensed). Personas run over literature_pool.json + openreview_extracts.

theorist.gap: Latent-thought training targets are ad-hoc (regression to frozen vision features, answer-conditioned posteriors with shortcut leakage per AMVL); no account of what information a latent step must preserve.
theorist.why_interesting: A principled objective would tell us when latent reasoning should work and when it cannot.
theorist.fix_sketch: Information-sufficiency formulation: latent step must be sufficient for the answer, minimal w.r.t. answer-shortcuts; testable via matched-information controls.

theorist.gap: Internal hallucination scores (hidden-state cosine, NTK quantities, SAE features) are asserted, not validated; reviewers explicitly note the causal link is missing.
theorist.why_interesting: A validated signal would unify a fragmented mitigation literature.
theorist.fix_sketch: Interventional test battery: manipulate signal, observe hallucination delta under confound controls. NOTE phase4_recheck: partially occupied by arxiv:2605.13156, arxiv:2606.08777.

theorist.gap: All multimodal latent-reasoning methods use fixed latent length / uniform computation regardless of instance difficulty or perceptual ambiguity.
theorist.why_interesting: Adaptive depth is both an efficiency and a scientific question: how much latent computation does visual reasoning need?
theorist.fix_sketch: Learned halting on evidence-sufficiency; formal stopping criterion; per-instance depth analysis.

theorist.gap: No formal decomposition of multimodal task error into perception vs reasoning components; RL papers claim 'reasoning gains' without an estimator that separates the two.
theorist.why_interesting: Decomposition converts leaderboard deltas into scientific claims about what RL changes.
theorist.fix_sketch: Oracle-perception interventions (ground-truth captions/detections) define an identifiable error accounting; track components across RL training.

engineer.gap: Latent reasoning promises efficiency but papers rarely report wall-clock/memory; interleaved image-generating CoT is extremely expensive (ThinkMorph review).
engineer.why_interesting: Decode-side cost is the deployable win; honest accounting could flip conclusions.
engineer.fix_sketch: Report accuracy-vs-decode-FLOPs/wall-clock Pareto with matched budgets.

engineer.gap: Hallucination fixes add multi-pass decoding / NTK computations with unreported wall-clock+memory overhead.
engineer.fix_sketch: Overhead-audited mitigation; amortize signal computation into the forward pass.

engineer.gap: Visual-token importance heuristics (attention, key-norm) fail matched-budget tests — random pruning ties sophisticated metrics (withdrawn Random-Visual-Tokens paper); signals unreliable.
engineer.why_interesting: Field-wide contradiction: either metrics are broken or evals are.
engineer.fix_sketch: Matched-budget benchmark of importance signals; explains when importance matters.

engineer.gap: Multimodal RL results ride on large rollout compute; single-backbone (Qwen-VL) monoculture; reproduction on modest hardware not demonstrated.
engineer.why_interesting: Attribution and reproducibility both suffer; small-scale controlled RL is missing.
engineer.fix_sketch: 3B-scale controlled GRPO studies with public configs.

skeptic.gap: Latent-reasoning evaluation is confounded: single dataset, text-only fine-tuning stage drives most of the gain (LVR Table 4 per review), unclear baseline data access — 'thinking in latents' never isolated from 'extra training'.
skeptic.why_interesting: The field's headline claims may be artifacts; a controlled protocol is itself a contribution.
skeptic.fix_sketch: Matched-data/matched-compute controls; ablate latent steps vs equivalent text tokens vs no-op padding.

skeptic.gap: Emergence claims rest on 2-5% frequency events also present in base model.
skeptic.fix_sketch: Base-model frequency controls for any 'emergent' behavior claim.

skeptic.gap: Mitigation evaluated on QA-style object-hallucination sets only; side effects measured nowhere (CASAL: OOD refusal up, accuracy down).
skeptic.why_interesting: A mitigation that trades coverage for precision is not a mitigation; the tax must be measured.
skeptic.fix_sketch: Side-effect audit suite: refusal rate, informativeness, general-ability retention, long-form.

skeptic.gap: RL gains marginal vs plain GRPO; unclear component attribution; visual forgetting after text-heavy RL unmeasured.
skeptic.why_interesting: The field may be re-measuring GRPO repeatedly.
skeptic.fix_sketch: Error-accounting before/after RL; vision-only regression suite.

pm.gap: Decode-side generation dominates edge VLM energy/latency ('Seeing is Free, Speaking is Not') while the field optimizes visual tokens; verbose CoT is the user-visible cost.
pm.why_interesting: Cutting reasoning tokens (latent/adaptive) is the product win; visual-token pruning is not.
pm.fix_sketch: Methods that reduce generated reasoning tokens with accuracy parity; report latency.

pm.gap: Multi-pass mitigation adds latency nobody will pay for.
pm.why_interesting: Single-pass or amortized mitigation is deployable.

pm.gap: Latent reasoning is unauditable — no inspectable intermediate states for debugging or trust.
pm.why_interesting: Adaptive halting + optional decodability gives a dial between cost and auditability.
pm.fix_sketch: Halting probes / decodable checkpoints as an interpretability hook.
